description:
Advances: mutagen.mutators

Deliverable: migrate .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md to the SpecLedEx schema:
- spec-scenarios block: reshape each scenario to given:/when:/then: arrays (bespoke shape fails parse with 'expected array').
- spec-verification block: replace execute:/source_file: stubs with command: and target: entries; every requirement gets a verification entry.
- spec-meta: flip status: draft -> active once the file validates.
- keep prose, intent, id, surface, and decisions links intact.

Verification:
- mix spec.validate emits ZERO errors for mutagen.mutators (baseline: 6 errors on this file).
- subject status is active in .spec/state.json.

Out of Scope (files): every .spec.md EXCEPT mutators.spec.md; lib/**; test/**.
Allowed touches: .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md only.
Out of Scope (intent): no realized_by bindings (phase2); no code/test edits; do not alter requirement ids or delete requirements.
Merge gate: mix spec.validate (0 errors for mutagen.mutators) && mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED

Migrated .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md to the SpecLedEx schema (commit 0bb8623 on mutagen-835.2.6/migrate-mutators).

Changes:
- spec-scenarios: reshaped all 7 scenarios (s1-s7) from bespoke scalar given:/when:/then: to given:/when:/then: arrays. Entries containing ': ' / flow-mapping syntax (e.g. 'do: x + 1', '{:ok, a}') use '- |' block scalars to avoid YAML misparsing as nested mappings.
- spec-verification: renamed command: -> target: on all 5 entries (v1-v5); kept execute: true. Added r7 to v1's covers so every requirement has a verification entry (closed-catalog requirement is observable via mutators_test.exs).
- spec-meta: status draft -> active.
- Prose, intent, id, surface, decisions links, requirement ids untouched. No realized_by added (phase2). No lib/** or test/** edits.
- Regenerated .spec/state.json committed alongside.

Merge gate (all pass locally):
- mix spec.validate: ZERO errors AND zero warnings for mutagen.mutators; subject status is 'active' in .spec/state.json. (Other unmigrated subjects still error - expected this phase.)
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean.
- mix test: 640 tests, 0 failures (19 excluded).

Allowed touches respected: only .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md + generated .spec/state.json (git status confirms).

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 0eb050fa-440a-426e-9f51-bfd4d1ee50de
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/0eb050fa-440a-426e-9f51-bfd4d1ee50de.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2 (.spec/specs/mutators.spec.md, .spec/state.json)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0 (state.json is the generated index, exempt)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (no other *.spec.md, no lib/**, no test/**)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 5, passed: 5
  - v1 mutators_test.exs: 15 tests, 0 failures
  - v2 id_stability_test.exs: 4 tests, 0 failures
  - v3 mutators_property_test.exs: 11 tests, 0 failures
  - v4 mutators/ --only validate: 26 tests, 0 failures
  - v5 case_drop_classification_test.exs: 4 tests, 0 failures
- Requirements coverage (one line per must requirement):
  - r1: covered by v1 (mutators_test.exs, 'every catalog module exports match?/1, mutate/1, validate/1'). Would fail if a mutator dropped one of the three callbacks.
  - r2: covered by v1 + v4 (--only validate). Would fail if a validate/1 rejection reason or mutation.skipped recording regressed.
  - r3: covered by v2 (id_stability_test.exs). Would fail if site ID shape {file}:{hash}:{name} or phash2 normalization changed.
  - r4: covered by v2. Would fail if enumerator site-ID set diverged across mix format.
  - r5: covered by v3 (property test). Would fail if mutate(mutate(node)) != node for a symmetric swap.
  - r6: covered by v3. Would fail if Macro.to_string(mutate(ast)) produced source Code.string_to_quoted rejected.
  - r7: covered by v1 ('catalog (mutagen.mutators.r7)' describe: assert length(Mutators.all()) == 10 and all() == canonical list). Would fail if an 11th mutator/plugin path were added or the closed catalog shrank.
  - r8: covered by v5 (case_drop_classification_test.exs). Would fail if guarded-recursive-base-case :case_drop classified as :timeout instead of :killed.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. No realized_by bindings added; no requirement ids altered/deleted (r/s/v id sets byte-identical to base); no lib/** or test/** edits; prose/intent/surface/decisions links preserved.
- Project verification: pass (mix spec.validate emits ZERO findings for mutagen.mutators; 135 corpus findings are all on unmigrated subjects scope_resolution/test_selection, expected this phase). Merge gate: mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix test 640 tests, 0 failures.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- spec-verification target: rename (was command:) matches the already-migrated cli.spec.md and json_schema.spec.md exactly. Sound.
- Adding r7 to v1's covers (vs a new entry) is correct: mutators_test.exs already had a describe block named for r7 asserting the closed ten-mutator catalog. Legitimate coverage, not a paper binding.
- All 7 scenarios reshaped to given/when/then arrays parse cleanly (spec.validate reports zero scenario errors for mutators); block-scalar '- |' entries containing ': '/flow-mapping syntax (s2, s7) parse correctly.
- All 8 requirements (r1-r8) have a covering verification entry; no verification entry references a nonexistent requirement.
- .spec/state.json shows no drift after re-running mix spec.validate (committed == regenerated); subject status confirmed 'active'.

title: migrate mutagen.mutators to SpecLedEx schema (6 errors)
